keyRules: Max. 120 m above ground level (EASA Open Category)
Register as a UAS operator if the drone weighs 250 g or more or is equipped with a camera
A1/A3 online course required — minimum age 16
Mandatory liability insurance — minimum coverage amount depends on drone weight; drones weighing less than 250 g are exempt from the minimum coverage requirement under BEK 527 (July 2025)
Copenhagen Airport's CTR covers central Copenhagen — check out Droneregler.dk
Always maintain visual line of sight (VLOS) with the drone
Do not fly over assemblies of people. Rules for uninvolved persons depend on subcategory, class mark and operating conditions; check the official matrix.
At least 8 km to IMC airports (instrument flight), 4 km to VMC airports — BEK 527 (July 2025)
Night flight is permitted under the EASA open-category baseline when the required green flashing light is used, unless a current geographic zone or national rule imposes a stricter condition.
Greenland and the Faroe Islands have separate rules
importantNotes: The minimum insurance coverage is now weight-dependent; drones weighing less than 250 g are exempt from the minimum coverage requirement as of BEK 527 (July 2025). UAS operator registration is now valid for 3 years.
Copenhagen Airport's CTR covers most of central Copenhagen
Greenland and the Faroe Islands: separate regulations apply
Fines and criminal prosecution may be imposed for violations
The GDPR applies when recognizable images are taken

fields.disclaimerText: Always check the Danish Transport Authority’s official sources and Droneregler.dk before flying. This app is a tool—not a legal authority. Denmark follows EASA regulations with national additions.
